title: Vendor Fleet Remediation Playbook
description: Run a vendor-funded fleet remediation: prove a bad population with rigorous testing, engage manufacturer for full remediation scope, require on-site vendor resources and cost ownership, and execute a time-boxed install-and-validate campaign with your support engineers.

mindset.principle: Paying through support tickets forever is the expensive path. Prove the batch, then make the vendor carry the fleet fix.

mistakes.label: Keep replacing units one by one under normal support
mistakes.cost: Customer down time stacks; your cost and brand burn

mistakes.label: Demand fleet replace without joint deep test
Your team does the whole campaign on your dime
Swap everything with no test plan

campaign_phases.label: Persistent site/fleet errors not explained by one-off fails
Deep test with vendor; isolate batch/population
Scope: all affected units; vendor funds parts + on-site labor
Time-boxed install + validation; your leads + their crew
Error rate gate; written close with vendor

decision_branches.situation: Constant errors across many systems in one environment
Vendor agrees to remediate at their cost

steps.name: Commercial scope and cost ownership
steps.goal: Vendor funds parts and on-site labor

agent_signal.use_when: Fleet or data-center scale failures. Bad batch suspected. Need deep test with vendor then full remediation at manufacturer expense with on-site joint install and validation.
decision_criteria: Vendor funds parts and on-site labor when batch is theirs.
Your team runs install plan and test gates; not silent ticket soak.
agent_signal.operator_context: Field pattern: DC-wide memory batch, week-long replace-and-test, vendor engineers on site, cost on manufacturer.
agent_signal.not_for: Single-unit RMA. Not legal contract drafting with vendors.
trigger_phrases: Fleet or data-center scale failures. Bad batch suspected. Need deep test with ve
